positioning: AI-native product engineer who ships trustworthy full-stack SaaS workflows with real backend logic, permission boundaries, operational evidence, and failure handling.

capture_method.recommended_next: Use a clean browser profile or Playwright storage state after user-approved demo sign-in. Do not store or print credentials.
capture_method.reason: Dashboard routes require authentication. Public live checks can verify deployment health, but protected screenshots need approved demo session state.

verified_public_checks.note: Public self-health endpoint only. Timing from this manual network check is not a product performance claim.
Controllable demo health modes are disabled in production and should not be used as public live evidence.

verified_public_checks.result: Protected dashboard routes render a redirect to /signin when no session is present; /signin is public and shows seeded demo-account sign-in copy.

tests.result: 27 test files passed, 134 tests passed on 2026-06-28

steady_state_cover.required_state: Deployed dashboard service is Healthy after a real Owner/Admin manual check. Overview scheduler evidence clearly says Not configured because no scheduled run evidence exists.
steady_state_cover.current_gap: Public API health is verified, but signed-in dashboard state was not inspected. Existing local screenshots show scheduled monitoring Active and are not suitable for this cover state.

failure_handling_secondary.required_state: A valid HTTP 200 health response with valid version but latency above the 1500 ms threshold is classified Degraded.
failure_handling_secondary.public_use: secondary proof item only, never the cover state
failure_handling_secondary.current_gap: Code and tests prove the classifier. A safe live/demo persisted screenshot of this exact latency-driven degraded state still needs approval and repeat capture.

pages.title: Overview - healthy manual-check evidence with scheduler Not configured
capture_notes: Repeat capture after approved sign-in and fresh manual check.
Crop to app viewport.
Do not use older screenshots that show scheduler Active.

pages.title: Services - persisted status and last-check evidence
must_show: Status, last checked, last healthy, latest latency
No fabricated uptime or static telemetry
capture_notes: Repeat capture in clean state.
Crop or blur internal/local URLs if visible.
Use public-safe service names.

pages.title: Service detail - healthy result, latency, version, history, configuration summary
must_show: Configuration summary without private URLs or emails
capture_notes: Use the deployed dashboard service detail if it is public-safe.
Crop out audit rows or blur actor emails.
Repeat after the final healthy manual check.

pages.title: Service detail - secondary degraded latency proof
must_show: Degraded classification message caused by latency
capture_notes: Secondary proof only.
Requires a safe approved endpoint or demo service that produces slow valid health JSON.
After capture, restore the service to Healthy before capturing the cover state.

pages.title: Events - safe operational-event triage workflow
must_show: Persisted event row from an authenticated source
capture_notes: Capture only if actual demo evidence is available.
Blur or crop idempotency keys, lookup IDs, local source details, and any sensitive metadata.
Never show ingestion keys or authorization headers.

pages.title: Incidents - safe incident timeline from a real event
must_show: Incident created from an operational event
capture_notes: Capture only if actual incident demo evidence exists.
Current inspected final screenshot was empty, so do not include incidents yet.
Blur actor emails and internal IDs if timeline is shown.

pages.title: Readiness - evidence-based unavailable integrations
must_show: Expected and observed versions are on service records
capture_notes: Safe to capture after sign-in.
Crop to app viewport.
Use as honest scope boundary rather than a feature claim.

pages.title: Viewer role - read-only and denied mutation evidence
must_show: Read-only banner or disabled mutation control
capture_notes: Use a clean viewer demo session.
Crop user panel or ensure it contains no email address.
Use tests/server evidence for direct denied mutation attempts; do not show credentials.

do_not_capture: Passwords, credentials, API keys, event ingestion keys, scheduler secrets, environment variables, database URLs
Neon, Vercel, provider dashboards, terminals, logs, DevTools, source code, private repository URLs
Browser chrome, bookmarks, notifications, desktop taskbar, unrelated apps or tabs
Internal endpoints, scheduler secret configuration, full idempotency keys, lookup IDs, raw payloads with sensitive fields
Old screenshots showing scheduler Active when the intended proof state is Not configured
Claims of active scheduled monitoring, automated alerting, paging, customers, revenue, uptime, scale, or external integrations
